SURFACE: GET / redirects to /login; GET/POST /login with username,password; static /static/css/bootstrap.min.css, /static/css/style.css, /static/js/bootstrap.bundle.min.js; common probes /robots.txt /api /admin /debug /healthz /search /sitemap.xml /.git/HEAD /static/ /assets/ /favicon.ico returned Flask 404. BUG-HYP: Authenticated area after test:test may expose a template-rendered input field; /login comment discloses valid test account, and category is SSTI. Need map post-login routes before attacking. WHY: /login HTML comment says TODO testing account (test:test); response bodies match Flask/Jinja ecosystem, where SSTI is plausible; no source files are present. PLAN: - HYPOTHESIS: Login with disclosed test:test reveals additional endpoints/forms, one field is rendered through Jinja without escaping/eval sandbox (evidence: valid credential comment + Flask-like stack + SSTI category). - ATTACK CHAIN: authenticate as test → harvest authenticated markup/JS/routes → find reflected/stored template field → arithmetic SSTI fingerprint → Jinja object graph/RCE or config leak → read flag from env/file via network. - FIRST PROBE: curl with cookie jar POST /login as test:test, then GET authenticated pages and linked assets. - ABANDON IF: test:test is invalid and no new routes/session pages appear; then switch to endpoint fuzzing without auth.
